title: podman assumes `/sbin` in path
body:
Example: 
```

$ podman run --rm -it fedora:28 echo "Hello world!"
error creating libpod runtime: could not initialize iptables protocol 0: exec: "iptables": executable file not found in $PATH
$ export PATH=$PATH:/usr/sbin
$ podman run --rm -it fedora:28 echo "Hello world!"
Hello world!


```
Oddly, before running this successfully the first time, I was getting the even more obscure error message `error creating libpod runtime: exit status 125`. Then when I added to path and ran _once_, that error has vanished replaced by this one.

comments.body: Hm. It's trying to initialize a firewall backend even though running rootless. That seems like a bug - we shouldn't even be hitting this code unless we have root

I just hit this issue as well:

```
[ngompa@localhost ~]$ podman pull registry.openshift.localhost.localdomain/fedora-obsbuild
could not get runtime: could not initialize iptables protocol 0: exec: "iptables": executable file not found in $PATH
```
